---
title: "AI Tutorials"
subtitle: "A step-by-step coding curriculum — use AI to defeat AI."
sidebar: ai-curriculum
listing:
  contents:
    - level-1-base-r/index.qmd
    - level-2-dplyr/index.qmd
    - level-3-purrr/index.qmd
    - level-4-data-table/index.qmd
  type: grid
  sort: title asc
  fields: [title, description]
  grid-columns: 2
toc: false
---

## Why this curriculum exists

Models can draft R faster than you can type. They also **confidently confuse** `map()` with `map2()`, drop grouping variables, or simplify lists in ways that break your reef, fishery, telemetry, or biologging pipeline. The answer is not to avoid AI — it is to **think clearly enough** to catch the error before it becomes a supplementary figure.

That kind of clarity is what education research calls **transfer**: you learn a pattern in one context (synthetic transects) and apply it in another (IMOS detections, CATS bursts, catch records). *How People Learn* emphasises connecting new ideas to prior knowledge and getting rapid feedback ([Bransford et al., 2000](https://nap.nationalacademies.org/catalog/9853/how-people-learn-brain-mind-experience-and-school-expanded-edition)). Doing science with code is the same loop as doing science in the lab: **predict → execute → compare → revise** ([Klahr & Dunbar, 1988](https://doi.org/10.1016/0166-4115(88)90004-2)).

::: {.quest-hub}
<p class="quest-hub__title">Four levels · 80 quests · ~10 minutes each</p>
<p class="quest-hub__hint">Open a level from the grid below or use the **Coding curriculum** sidebar to jump to any quest. Finish Level 1 before Level 3 — purrr builds on the split–apply habits you learn in base R and dplyr.</p>
:::

| Level | Focus | You will… |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| **1 · Base R** | Vectors, `if`/`else`, loops, `apply` family | Read any AI script without panicking at `[` or `lapply` |
| **2 · dplyr** | `mutate`, `group_by`, `lead`/`lag`, joins, pivots | Reshape ecological tables before modelling |
| **3 · purrr & furrr** | `map`, `walk`, `safely`, parallel maps | Replace fragile loops on tags, years, and bursts |
| **4 · data.table** | `[i, j, by]`, joins, `shift`, `fcase` | Handle large detection tables with less memory |

**R Tutorials** on this site are full workflows (telemetry, dive logs). **AI Tutorials** are the **coding curriculum** — reps that train critical thinking, not copy-paste.

Pick **Level 1 · Base R** if AI keeps handing you `apply` and you nod but cannot rewrite it.
